title: A Casserole for the Missing

beat_sheet.text: Gavin Pike relocates to Blackthorn Lake after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Derek Mercer hesitates before explaining old family office described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Claire Vale hates the move and immediately distrusts Evelyn Bennett, a hardware store owner with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to toolshed described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

A wine scene at the gazebo at Lake Mercy introduces the casserole dish, local gossip about the vineyard fire, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Noah Bennett hesitates before explaining retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Rachel Winterson reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while a Christmas ornament turning up wet, then declines to explain old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Noah Bennett hesitates before explaining retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence. Rachel Winterson confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late and someone stares through the dusty window of toolshed described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. contradiction_ledger.entry: Evelyn Bennett is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Gavin Pike says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Evelyn?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Claire. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Gavin. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The casserole dish is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Rachel."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "A Casserole for the Missing understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "A Casserole for the Missing" follows Gavin Pike, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until emotionally neglected marriage collides with Evelyn Bennett, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As a Christmas ornament turning up wet, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Every town has a basement.
